Title: Valid vendor login
Preconditions: Vendor account exists and is active
Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid email; 3. Enter valid password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: User is logged in and redirected to vendor dashboard

Title: Login with invalid password
Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid email; 3. Enter wrong password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Error 'Invalid credentials' shown; user remains on login page

Title: Login with non-existent email
Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter random email; 3. Enter random password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Generic 'Invalid credentials' error (no user-enumeration)

Title: Account lockout after repeated failed attempts
Steps: 1. Attempt login with wrong password 5 consecutive times; 2. Try with correct password
Test Data: Wrong password x5 then correct password
Expected Result: Account locked for cool-down; correct password also denied during lockout

Title: Session timeout after inactivity
Steps: 1. Login; 2. Leave session idle past configured timeout; 3. Click a protected action
Expected Result: User redirected to login; protected action does not execute

Title: Password reset flow
Steps: 1. Click Forgot Password; 2. Enter email; 3. Open reset link; 4. Set new password; 5. Login with new password
Expected Result: Reset email received; new password accepted; old password is rejected

Title: Logout invalidates session
Steps: 1. Logout; 2. Use browser back to access protected page; 3. Re-use prior session token via API
Expected Result: Protected page not accessible; reused token returns 401

Title: Submit valid invoice linked to open PO
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; PO in OPEN status
Steps: 1. Go to Upload Invoice; 2. Select PO; 3. Enter invoice number/date/amount; 4. Attach valid PDF; 5. Submit
Expected Result: Invoice saved with status SUBMITTED and linked to PO

Title: Submit without selecting PO
Steps: 1. Skip PO selection; 2. Fill remaining fields; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked with validation 'PO is required'

Title: Submit with missing invoice number
Steps: 1. Fill PO and amount; 2. Leave invoice number blank; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Validation error shown; record not persisted

Title: Submit with invalid file format
Steps: 1. Fill all fields; 2. Attach .exe file; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Server rejects file with 'Unsupported format'

Title: Submit with file exceeding size limit
Steps: 1. Fill all fields; 2. Attach file larger than configured max; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Upload rejected with size-limit error

Title: Submit invoice amount greater than PO balance
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; PO balance < entered amount
Steps: 1. Select PO with balance 1000; 2. Enter invoice amount 2000; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked or routed to AP per business rule (see gap)

Title: Duplicate invoice number for same vendor
Preconditions: Invoice number already exists for vendor
Steps: 1. Submit invoice with number INV-100; 2. Submit again with same number INV-100
Expected Result: Second submission rejected with 'Duplicate invoice number'

Title: Submit against closed PO
Steps: 1. Select closed PO; 2. Fill all fields; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked with appropriate message

Title: Resubmit after rejection
Steps: 1. Open rejected invoice; 2. Edit fields per rejection reason; 3. Resubmit
Expected Result: Resubmission succeeds; status changes to SUBMITTED; audit log records resubmission

Title: AP approves a submitted invoice
Preconditions: Invoice in SUBMITTED status; AP user logged in
Steps: 1. Open invoice from AP queue; 2. Review details; 3. Click Approve; 4. Confirm
Expected Result: Status changes to APPROVED; approver and timestamp recorded; invoice moves to payment stage

Title: AP rejects a submitted invoice with reason
Preconditions: Invoice in SUBMITTED status; AP user logged in
Steps: 1. Open invoice; 2. Click Reject; 3. Enter rejection reason; 4. Confirm
Expected Result: Status changes to REJECTED; reason persisted; vendor notified

Title: Rejection without reason is blocked
Preconditions: Invoice in SUBMITTED status; AP user logged in
Steps: 1. Click Reject; 2. Leave reason blank; 3. Confirm
Expected Result: Validation error 'Rejection reason is required'; status unchanged

Title: Vendor cannot approve invoices
Preconditions: Vendor logged in with submitted invoice
Steps: 1. As vendor open own invoice; 2. Attempt approve via UI and API
Expected Result: UI does not expose Approve action; API returns 403 Forbidden

Title: Concurrent approval by two AP users
Preconditions: Two AP users have same invoice open
Steps: 1. AP-1 clicks Approve; 2. AP-2 clicks Reject within 1 sec
Expected Result: First action wins; second action receives conflict error; audit log shows both

Title: Rejected invoice does not proceed to payment
Steps: 1. Trigger payment scheduler; 2. Check payment queue
Expected Result: Rejected invoice is excluded from payment queue

Title: Approved invoice appears in payment queue
Steps: 1. Trigger payment scheduler; 2. Check payment queue
Expected Result: Invoice appears in payment queue exactly once

Title: Audit log captures approval/rejection
Steps: 1. Approve invoice A; 2. Reject invoice B; 3. Inspect audit log
Expected Result: Each action logged with actor user id timestamp action and reason

Title: Email to vendor on invoice submission
Preconditions: SMTP configured; vendor has valid email
Steps: 1. Vendor submits invoice; 2. Inspect mailbox
Expected Result: Email arrives within SLA; subject and body contain invoice number date and status SUBMITTED

Title: Email to vendor on approval
Steps: 1. AP approves invoice; 2. Inspect vendor mailbox
Expected Result: Email arrives with status APPROVED and link to invoice

Title: Email to vendor on rejection with reason
Steps: 1. AP rejects with reason; 2. Inspect vendor mailbox
Expected Result: Email arrives with status REJECTED reason included

Title: Email retry on SMTP failure
Steps: 1. Submit invoice while SMTP down; 2. Restore SMTP; 3. Wait for retry
Expected Result: Retry with backoff; email eventually delivered; failure logged

Title: Notification contains correct vendor data
Preconditions: Vendor has display name and email
Steps: 1. Trigger any status change email
Expected Result: Email addressed to correct vendor; contains correct invoice number amount and status

Title: Generate monthly invoice report
Steps: 1. Open Reports; 2. Select month; 3. Click Generate
Expected Result: Report generates and displays totals counts and breakdown by status

Title: Report counts match DB
Steps: 1. Generate report for month M; 2. Query DB for count by status WHERE month=M; 3. Compare
Expected Result: Counts in report equal counts in DB for every status

Title: Filter report by status
Steps: 1. Filter APPROVED; 2. Filter REJECTED; 3. Filter PENDING
Expected Result: Each filter returns only invoices in selected status

Title: Report respects role scope
Steps: 1. As vendor open Reports; 2. Generate report
Expected Result: Vendor only sees their own invoices; AP sees all

Title: Duplicate invoice upload (same file twice)
Steps: 1. Upload invoice file F; 2. Upload same file again with same invoice number
Expected Result: Second upload rejected as duplicate

Title: Interrupted upload of large file
Steps: 1. Begin upload of 9MB file; 2. Close browser at 50%; 3. Reopen and re-upload
Expected Result: No orphan partial record persists; re-upload succeeds; no duplicate created

Title: Session expires during submission
Steps: 1. Begin form; 2. Wait for session expiry; 3. Submit
Expected Result: User redirected to login; no orphan record persists

Title: Notification delivery failure
Steps: 1. Trigger event during outage; 2. Restore SMTP
Expected Result: Retry mechanism delivers email post-recovery; failure logged

Title: POST /api/auth/login returns 200 on valid credentials
Steps: 1. POST with valid email and password
Expected Result: 200; returns session token and user profile

Title: POST /api/auth/login returns 401 on wrong password
Steps: 1. POST with valid email and wrong password
Expected Result: 401; no token issued

Title: POST /api/invoices returns 201 on valid payload
Steps: 1. POST with po_id invoice_number amount file_id
Expected Result: 201; returns created invoice with id and status SUBMITTED

Title: POST /api/invoices returns 400 on missing field
Steps: 1. POST omitting po_id
Expected Result: 400; validation error listing missing field

Title: POST /api/invoices returns 401 without token
Steps: 1. POST valid payload without auth header
Expected Result: 401; resource not created

Title: GET /api/invoices/{id} cross-vendor returns 403/404
Steps: 1. GET id that belongs to Vendor B
Expected Result: 403 or 404 (404 preferred to avoid leaking existence)

Title: GET /api/invoices enforces vendor scoping
Steps: 1. GET without filters
Expected Result: 200; only invoices belonging to authenticated vendor returned

Title: POST /api/invoices/{id}/approve as vendor returns 403
Steps: 1. POST approve on own invoice
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden

Title: POST /api/invoices/{id}/reject with empty reason returns 400
Steps: 1. POST reject with empty reason
Expected Result: 400; validation error 'Reason is required'

Title: Expired token returns 401 on any endpoint
Steps: 1. Call any protected endpoint with expired token
Expected Result: 401; client must re-authenticate

Title: Invoice row created with correct vendor and PO mapping
Steps: SELECT id, vendor_id, po_id, status FROM invoices WHERE invoice_number='INV-100'
Expected Result: Single row; vendor_id matches submitter; po_id matches selected PO; status=SUBMITTED

Title: Approval persists status approver and timestamp
Steps: SELECT status, approved_by, approved_at FROM invoices WHERE id=<id>
Expected Result: status=APPROVED; approved_by populated; approved_at recent

Title: Rejection persists status reason and rejector
Steps: SELECT status, rejection_reason, rejected_by, rejected_at FROM invoices WHERE id=<id>
Expected Result: status=REJECTED; rejection_reason not null; rejected_by and timestamp populated

Title: Every status change written to audit log
Steps: SELECT * FROM invoice_audit_log WHERE invoice_id=<id> ORDER BY ts
Expected Result: One row per action (CREATE APPROVE REJECT RESUBMIT) with actor and timestamp

Title: Vendor isolation enforced in queries
Steps: Inspect app SQL via query log
Expected Result: Application queries always include vendor_id filter

Title: Duplicate prevention via unique constraint
Steps: Attempt to INSERT duplicate (vendor_id invoice_number)
Expected Result: DB raises unique-constraint violation; application returns 409

Title: Invoice cannot reference non-existent PO
Steps: Attempt INSERT invoice with non-existent po_id
Expected Result: FK constraint blocks insert

Title: Report counts match DB counts
Steps: SELECT status, count(*) FROM invoices WHERE month=M GROUP BY status
Expected Result: Counts equal report counts exactly

Title: Vendor cannot approve (RBAC)
Steps: 1. Call approve endpoint with vendor token
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden; status unchanged

Title: Cross-vendor invoice access blocked
Steps: 1. Request invoice belonging to Vendor B
Expected Result: 403 or 404; no data returned

Title: IDOR on invoice file download
Steps: 1. Request file_id belonging to Vendor B's invoice
Expected Result: Access denied; file not served

Title: Brute-force login protection
Steps: 1. Send rapid wrong-password attempts from one IP
Expected Result: Rate limit or lockout triggers; alert logged

Title: Session token cannot be reused after logout
Steps: 1. Logout; 2. Replay prior token via API
Expected Result: 401 Unauthorized

Title: Malicious file upload blocked
Steps: 1. Upload PDF with EICAR test string or executable content
Expected Result: File rejected or quarantined; failure logged

Title: File content-type spoofing blocked
Steps: 1. Rename .exe to .pdf and upload
Expected Result: Server detects mime mismatch and rejects
